title: sorbus mayeri
intro: Sorbus mayeri, also known as the Korean Mountain Ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Korea and China, belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant. Sorbus mayeri can be prepared in various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
